title: Extract config to JSON and add awesome-repo paper sources

stories.title: Extract hardcoded config to JSON file
acceptanceCriteria: A config.json file exists at project root with keys: author_queries, priority_authors, priority_orgs, search_queries, categories, paper_categories
config.json includes the two new authors: Arhan Jain and Abhishek Gupta in both author_queries and priority_authors
config.json includes an awesome_repos list with 4 repos: jonyzhang2023/awesome-embodied-vla-va-vln, yueen-ma/Awesome-VLA, Psi-Robot/Awesome-VLA-Papers, Denghaoyuan123/Awesome-RL-VLA
daily_briefing.py loads all config from config.json instead of hardcoding lists
No hardcoded author/org/query lists remain in daily_briefing.py (except as fallback defaults)

stories.title: Implement awesome-repo paper fetching
acceptanceCriteria: A function exists that fetches README.md from each awesome repo via GitHub raw URL
The function parses arxiv paper links from the README content
Only papers updated/added within the last 2 weeks are included (based on git commit history of the repo)
Parsed papers are integrated into the collect_papers() pipeline with deduplication
Papers from awesome repos are scored and ranked alongside arXiv search results

stories.title: End-to-end verification
acceptanceCriteria: Python syntax check passes (python -m py_compile daily_briefing.py)
config.json is valid JSON and loads without errors
The Claude prompt in summarize_papers reflects the new authors (Arhan Jain, Abhishek Gupta)
All imports used in the new code are available (no missing dependencies)
